clause_ref: NFPA70E 2024:Table 130.7(C)(15)(b) — DC Equipment; Reference: Annex D (Doan method + Stokes & Oppenlander)
title: PPE Category for Common DC Tasks (Table Method)
description: Conservative PPE category assignment for DC electrical tasks when no detailed incident-energy analysis per NFPA 70E Annex D (Doan method) is available. Covers battery rooms, DC fast-charging systems, and photovoltaic (PV) arrays. Incident energy for DC is typically lower than equivalent-voltage AC systems but arc duration can be longer.

rows.task: Visual inspection of terminals (no contact, cover in place)
Remove/install connector or breaker (covers off, potential arc)
Work on cell interconnect (live, high-current path)
Access cabinet / replace filter (energized, no work inside)
Work on power-stage components or bus (energized)
Perform IV curve test (probe insertion into combiner)
Replace fuse / breaker (combiner de-energised via disconnect)
Work on combiner main switch (energized, potential arc on open)
Replace battery module (monitor disconnected, no LOTO)
Service main disconnect (energized, high parallel current path)
Service contactor / pre-charge resistor (LOTO + mechanical isolation applied)
Service battery pack connectors (energized, no LOTO)

notes: DC arc-flash analysis requires the Doan method (NFPA 70E Annex D.1) combined with Stokes & Oppenlander arc-voltage characteristics (Annex D.2). DC arcs tend to self-extinguish faster than AC due to commutation, but clearing times and arc voltages must be calculated, not assumed. Short-circuit current shown is the available fault current on the DC bus; DC source impedance is much lower than AC systems. This table is conservative; actual PPE category should be computed per Annex D when detailed system parameters are known.
license_note: DC task and PPE mapping derived from NFPA 70E:2024 public table structure and Annex D reference methods.
